titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Juanita Moore Papers
titlestmt.author: by Janet Lopes

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday November 27, 2024 at 23:58

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 10 (born digital): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Langdon Manor Books, LLC on the Sinclair Lewis Fund, 2023.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into five series: I. Scripts, 1958-1991. II. Personal and professional papers, 1961-2005. III. Photographs, 1930-2004. IV. Awards, 1958-2004.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Juanita Moore (1914-2014) was born in Greenwood, Mississippi and spent a majority of her life as a dancer, actress, and stage performer. She appeared in more than 70 movies and numerous plays during her 60-year career which began at the age of 17 as a dancer. Moore is best known for her role as Annie Johnson, an African-American housekeeper in the 1959 film Imitation of Life . It earned her an Academy Award nomination, only the third black actress to be nominated for the Supporting Actress category at the time. Moore turned to activism and refused to play maids or similiar roles following her nomination. She appeared in several TV shows in the 1960s and pursued theater work in addition to her work on the screen. In the 1980s, Moore was a founding member of the Cambridge Players, a black theater troupe. Moore worked well into the early 2000s. She died at her Los Angeles home at the age of 99.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Juanita Moore Papers. James Weldon Johnson Collection in the Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Juanita Moore papers contain correspondence, scripts, photographs, awards, printed material, electronic files, and other papers created by or relating to Juanita Moore. The collection includes seven scripts; most notable is the revised final screenplay for Imitation of Life . Moore played Annie Johnson in her Academy Award-nominated role in the 1959 film. Other highlights include documents related to the Cambridge Players, a black theater troupe founded, in part, by Moore. Close to 600 photographs capture Moore's life both personally and professionally. Prints include performances and promotions as well as images of Moore in her 20s and 30s alongside family and friends. The collection also includes several awards, one of them from the Black Filmmakers Hall of Fame.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Juanita Moore papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series are arranged chronologically by date of creation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains scripts and related materials.

did.unittitle: Made to Order , script adaptation and laid in photographic print

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into two sub-series: I. Personal papers, 1978-2005; and II. Professional papers, 1961-2002.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, pamphlets, photographs, computer media, and other materials related to Juanita Moore's life and work.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 10 (born digital): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.p: Box 10 (born digital): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Theater of Life proposal, Glenn Towery
Junior Black Academy, programs, pamphlets, and correspondence
Programs, composite cards, correspondence and other papers

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series are largely arranged in chronological order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains publicity, professional, and personal photographs related to Juanita Moore's life and work, along with related materials.

did.unittitle: Black and white photographic prints of family and friends
Event at Sally Stanford restaurant, album
Publicity, promotional, and other 8 x 10 prints
Color photographic prints of family and friends
Black and white photographic prints from Isabelle Cooley
Celebrity party with Don L. Lathrop
Award presentation for husband Charles T. Burris
Photographic prints in color of family and friends
45th anniversary cast reunion of Imitation of Life
Tour of the TRW plant with husband

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains awards and certificates related to Juanita Moore's life and work, along with related materials.

did.unittitle: Papers related to the 32nd annual Academy Awards
Black Filmmakers Hall of Fame award
Jeanie Golden Halo Award of High Esteem
